title: Maharashtrian-spiced Indian chewy ferment Chaat
description: A buttery, chewy, and cozy Indian dish from the state of Maharashtra, featuring a unique blend of five spice powder and a ferment.

ingredients.name: Yeast
ingredients.notes: Active dry yeast, instant for faster fermentation.

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the rice flour, yeast, and warm water to create the dough.
Mix the dough thoroughly until it forms a sticky ball.
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest in a warm place for 2-3 hours, allowing it to ferment and rise.
After fermentation, knead the dough gently for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Heat oil in a dolsot-stone-bowl (or a non-stick pan) over medium heat.
Add the ghee or unsalted butter and let it melt.
Add the five spice powder, turmeric powder, red chili powder, and salt, and mix well.
Add the fermented dough and mix until it becomes a uniform, buttery mixture.
Add the ginger paste, green chili paste, and mix well.
Add the desiccated coconut and mix well (if using).
Transfer the mixture to the preheated bowl or pan and cook over medium heat for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it is cooked through and has a slightly crispy bottom.
Garnish with fresh cilantro, lime juice, and serve hot with yogurt and chaat masala, if desired.
Optional: Top with puffed rice for added crunch.
